description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a light gray exterior and a steep, shingled hip roof. The house features symmetrical rows of rectangular windows on all sides, with a prominent bay window section on one end and a large chimney on the opposite side. The front facade includes a central door beneath a window, flanked by evenly spaced windows, and a small patch of green lawn. The roof is covered with weathered gray shingles, giving it a traditional, slightly rustic appearance. The structure appears to be made primarily of wood, with glass windows and a masonry chimney. The overall design suggests a Colonial Revival style with some Victorian influences, especially in the bay window and roof detailing.
description: A two-story light gray house with a steep shingled roof, symmetrical windows, a bay window section, and a prominent chimney.
description_view_0: Side view showing three evenly spaced windows on each floor and the edge of the green lawn.
description_view_1: Front view with the main entry door, symmetrical window arrangement, and a green lawn leading up to the house.
description_view_2: Opposite side view featuring a large chimney, three windows per floor, and a partial view of the lawn.
description_view_3: Rear view highlighting the bay window section, rear entry door, and the chimney extending up the side.
attribute_entryway_design: Central door with rectangular transom window, no porch, simple trim, and direct access to lawn
